(sha=bf213ac5c4a67dd92b96652fe52c170446e567da)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands MiMo automatic cookie import beyond Chrome variants, broadens Mistral default cookie probing to the provider order, updates provider error/docs/settings copy, and adds focused browser-order tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main hard-codes MiMo Auto to Chrome variants, and this PR changes it to Safari, Chrome variants, Firefox, and Edge. I did not run live browser-cookie import because AGENTS.md warns against validation that can display Keychain prompts unless explicitly requested.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining work needs contributor-side real browser proof and maintainer judgment on Mistral's browser-cookie scope; automation cannot safely supply either for this PR.

target.title: Fix Chrome-only browser cookie import and misleading copy across providers
target.body:
## Summary

Several providers on `main` either **only imported Chrome cookies in Auto mode** or **told users to sign in in Chrome** even when the importer/descriptor already supported Safari, Firefox, Edge, and other browsers. This PR fixes that mismatch across the affected providers.

Closes #1304 (reported on MiMo, same root cause).

## Fixes by provider

| Provider | What was wrong | What changed |
|----------|----------------|--------------|
| **MiMo** | Auto import hard-coded to Chrome / Chrome Beta / Chrome Canary only; settings + docs said “Chrome browser cookies” | Curated Auto order: Safari, Chrome variants, Firefox, Edge; updated settings copy, locales, `docs/mimo.md` |
| **Mistral** | Descriptor advertises full browser order, but importer defaulted to Chrome-only at runtime | Importer default now uses the provider browser order |
| **Command Code** | Full browser import order in code, error message still said “Sign in … in Chrome” | Error message uses provider `loginHint` |
| **Alibaba Token Plan** | Multi-browser descriptor, error message still said “Sign in to Bailian in Chrome” | Error message uses `loginHint` |
| **Ollama** | Chrome-first + fallback in code; docs read Chrome-only | `docs/ollama.md` aligned with actual behavior |
| **Augment** | Docs listed wrong browser priority vs descriptor | `docs/augment.md` aligned with descriptor |

## Unchanged on purpose

| Provider | Reason |
|----------|--------|
| **Grok**, **OpenCode** (non-Go) | Deliberate Chrome-only Auto import |
| **Ollama**, **Abacus** (code) | Chrome-first then fallback — AGENTS.md pattern; Ollama docs only updated here |
| **Windsurf**, **MiniMax** | Chromium localStorage importers, not generic cookie import |

## Example: MiMo Auto browser order (#1304)

```swift
// main
[.chrome, .chromeBeta, .chromeCanary]

// this PR
[.safari, .chrome, .chromeBeta, .chromeCanary, .firefox, .edge]
```

Other Chromium forks (Arc, Brave, …) for MiMo remain **Manual** — not the full SweetCookieKit default order.

## Validation

```bash
swift test --filter "BrowserCookieOrderStatusStringTests|MiMoProviderTests"
make check
```

- 31 focused tests pass (browser-order regressions for MiMo, Mistral, Command Code, Alibaba Token Plan + MiMo provider tests)
- 0 lint violations

Live browser cookie import not run (AGENTS.md — avoids Keychain prompts). Stubbed MiMo web-strategy tests cover the import → fetch path without live browser reads.

## Scope (23 files)

- **Core:** `Providers.swift`, `MiMoProviderDescriptor.swift`, `MistralCookieImporter.swift`, `CommandCodeUsageError.swift`, `AlibabaTokenPlanSettingsReader.swift`
- **UI / i18n:** `MiMoProviderImplementation.swift`, 11 locale files
- **Docs:** `mimo.md`, `ollama.md`, `alibaba-token-plan.md`, `augment.md`
- **Tests:** `BrowserCookieOrderLabelTests.swift`
